description: Frozen trace-summary v1 contract for the agent-delivery-harness (issue #98). Machine-readable authority for the per-issue run summary emitted by scripts/trace-report.sh to <trace dir>/trace-summary.json (idempotent overwrite, never append). This is the input contract for #104 (cross-run scorecard). Sibling of trace-schema.v1.json, which governs the trace.jsonl spans the summary is computed from.

notes.absence_semantics: null means no data; 0 means measured zero; [] means the detector ran and found nothing. A metric is never fabricated from absent spans (e.g. no model spans => tokens is null, never 0).
notes.open_world: Additive top-level keys are allowed in later v1.x revisions; existing keys and their semantics are frozen. Optional keys such as coverage, skills, bounded, and closed_by may be absent from older v1 summaries. Consumers reject unknown majors of summary_schema_version.
notes.single_source_of_numbers: The markdown report on stdout is rendered FROM this object, so the human report and this machine summary can never disagree.
notes.computed_only: Every value is computed from spans on disk; nothing is self-reported. Lines that do not parse as a JSON object are excluded from every aggregate and counted in span_counts.invalid_lines (validation itself is scripts/validate-trace.sh's job).
notes.visibility: Local-only, never committed; covered by the existing .gitignore rule .copilot-tracking/issues/issue-*/.

fields.trace_file: string; the trace.jsonl path the summary was computed from.
fields.issue: number or null; harness.issue carried through from the spans on disk (first numeric value seen), never invented locally.
fields.harness_versions: array of unique, sorted strings; every harness.version value seen on the spans, carried through, never invented locally. When multiple versions appear (harness upgraded mid-issue), consumers decide the bucketing; recommended: attribute the run to the last harness version seen.
fields.finished: boolean; true iff a finish lifecycle step exists anywhere in the trace.
fields.final_outcome: string or null; harness.outcome of the LAST finish span; null when the run is unfinished (finished false).
fields.bounded: boolean; true iff a terminal close edge exists from either a finish lifecycle step or a pr_merge lifecycle step. Additive open-world optional key; not in required_top_level, so older summaries without it remain valid. Does not redefine finished, which remains finish-only.
fields.closed_by: string or null; "finish" when any finish lifecycle step exists, else "pr_merge" when any pr_merge lifecycle step exists, else null for an open/unbounded run. Finish takes precedence when both close edges appear. Additive open-world optional key; not in required_top_level, so older summaries without it remain valid. final_outcome remains finish-only and is not inferred from pr_merge.
fields.span_counts: object {total, invalid_lines, by_type}; total counts aggregated span objects, invalid_lines counts skipped lines (non-JSON or JSON-non-object), by_type maps span type to count. Measured zeros are real zeros.
fields.coverage: object {has_tool_spans, has_model_spans}; booleans stating whether the run captured per-tool-call spans and token-bearing model spans (both originate in the runtime adapter, so their presence is the instrumentation level of the run). Additive open-world optional key (issue #131), not in required_top_level: an older summary without it is still valid. Lets the cross-run scorecard segment instrumented from lifecycle-only runs instead of blending them; the span-kind counts themselves ride span_counts.by_type.
fields.wall_clock: object {first_timestamp, last_timestamp, elapsed_seconds} or null when no span carries a timestamp; elapsed_seconds is first-to-last timestamp elapsed (whole-run wall clock, includes agent thinking time between spans) — a different clock from the summed duration_ms figures, never blended with them.
fields.stages: array of {step, spans, duration_ms}; one entry per harness.lifecycle_step seen across ALL span types (steps ride on agent spans too). duration_ms is the per-stage sum of harness.duration_ms, or null when none of the stage's spans carry one (null = no data, 0 = measured zero).
fields.tools: array of {name, calls, fail_calls, duration_ms} keyed on gen_ai.tool.name; duration_ms sums harness.duration_ms or is null when absent.
fields.feature_delivery: optional object {rows, coverage} derived from lifecycle steps and feature ids, independent of agent role. rows contains one {id, start_timestamp, green_timestamp, elapsed_seconds, final_green_outcome, blocked_green_count} for each feature with a first observed feature_start timestamp and a later green_handback; the last observed later GREEN supplies the paired edge and final outcome. elapsed_seconds includes all time between those recorded edges and makes no tool-call attribution claim. coverage is {paired, of}, where of counts distinct non-empty feature ids with feature_start. An unpaired or invalid-timestamp feature contributes to of but has no row and no zero elapsed value. Additive open-world optional key (issue #296), not in required_top_level.
fields.review_verdicts: optional object {pass, fail, blocked, total, fail_rate} derived from every observed review_verdict lifecycle step, independent of agent role. total is the observed denominator; fail_rate is null when total is 0. Additive open-world optional key (issue #296), not in required_top_level.
fields.green_handbacks: optional object {pass, fail, blocked, total, blocked_rate} derived from every observed green_handback lifecycle step, independent of agent role. total is the observed denominator; blocked_rate is null when total is 0. Additive open-world optional key (issue #296), not in required_top_level.
fields.skills: array of {name, calls, fail_calls} keyed on harness.skill.name (issue #139): which skills were invoked (loaded) and how many times, fail_calls counting load failures. Additive open-world optional key; [] when no skill spans. A skill span is a tool span whose gen_ai.tool.name is 'skill'; this projection keeps a skill's load count from being buried inside the generic 'skill' entry of tools[].
fields.tokens: null when the trace has no model spans carrying usage numbers (token data unavailable — absence is null, never 0; a model span without usage fields contributes nothing rather than fabricating zero buckets); when usage-carrying model spans exist, an object with input/output sums and attribution buckets, with unresolvable attribution under "unattributed".
fields.loop_indicators: array of {signature, count}, sorted by signature; deterministic exact-repeat detector. Identity = the full span object MINUS the volatile fields span_id, parent_span_id, timestamp, harness.duration_ms, harness.version (loop detection measures within-run thrash; a harness upgrade mid-burst must neither split a group nor produce duplicate signatures — cross-run version comparison is #104's job); groups with count >= 3 are reported. signature is "/"-joined: span, (gen_ai.tool.name or harness.lifecycle_step), harness.outcome when present, harness.stage when present — e.g. lifecycle/pr_merge/fail/ci_checks. [] means the detector ran and found nothing.
fields.red_reentry: array of harness.feature_id values that saw a red_handback AFTER an earlier green_handback in file order, counting harness.lifecycle_step across ALL span types. [] means none.
fields.deviations: object {count, feature_ids}; counts harness.lifecycle_step == "deviation" across ALL span types; feature_ids lists the unique harness.feature_id values on those spans. count 0 with feature_ids [] is a measured zero.
fields.log_failures: object {total, by_stage} or null (issue #221); the gate-failure surface derived from the sibling log.jsonl (the detail stream, docs/evaluation/log-schema.v1.json) beside the trace. Additive open-world optional key; NOT in required_top_level, so summaries built without a log.jsonl remain valid. null = no readable sibling log.jsonl (log evidence unavailable — absence is null, never a fabricated 0). When the log is read: total counts only gate-failure records (level == "error" AND harness.outcome == "fail"), by_stage groups those by harness.stage into {stage: count}. total 0 with by_stage {} is a MEASURED zero (the log was read and no failures were found), distinct from null.
